ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for a multi-component salmon platter combining sushi, grilled salmon, mentaiko salmon, and hanamaki. FOODON_03315595 (salmon food product) used as closest parent.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC (Salmon, preparations) used as closest parent. No specific code exists for a multi-component platter of this type.

serving.description: 1 platter (approximately 4 components: salmon sushi pieces, grilled caramelized salmon portion, grilled mentaiko salmon portion, hanamaki)
serving.natural_language_portion: One platter serving of approximately 380 g, comprising salmon sushi nigiri pieces, a grilled caramelized salmon fillet portion, a grilled mentaiko salmon portion, and hanamaki (steamed flower rolls), as served at Sushi Express Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central: Salmon, Atlantic, farmed, cooked, dry heat (FDC ID 175168); Sushi rice (FDC ID 169752); Mentaiko / pollock roe (FDC ID 175180); Steamed bun / hanamaki estimated from USDA FDC wheat-flour steamed bun analogue
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for this composite platter. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted component averaging: approximately 45% grilled/raw salmon, 35% sushi rice, 10% mentaiko, 10% hanamaki. USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy and Foundation Foods entries used for each component. Values represent a plausible per-100g composite estimate for the full platter.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — sushi rice and mixed sushi analogues; Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this composite platter. GI estimated at 54 (low) based on the dominant glycemic driver being sushi rice (GI ~55, University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson et al. 2021). The salmon and mentaiko components contribute negligible glycemic load. Weighted composite GI approximated at 54. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (54 × 62.7) / 100 ≈ 33.9, rounded to 33.7 g carbohydrate per 380 g serving × GI factor.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content from soy sauce, mentaiko, and seasoned sushi rice
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should be aware that this platter contains approximately 310 mg sodium per 100 g (approximately 1,178 mg per full 380 g serving), primarily from soy sauce, mentaiko seasoning, and seasoned sushi rice vinegar, which may approach or exceed 50% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2,000 mg; portion control and avoidance of additional soy sauce dipping is advised.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index driven by sushi rice; high-quality protein from salmon may attenuate postprandial glucose response
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should note that the sushi rice component contributes a low GI of approximately 54 and a glycemic load of approximately 34 per serving; the high-quality salmon protein (approximately 50 g per serving) may partially attenuate postprandial glucose excursion, but carbohydrate portion monitoring remains important, and pairing with non-starchy vegetables is recommended.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High omega-3 fatty acid content from salmon (EPA and DHA); moderate sodium from seasoning
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Salmon is a rich source of long-chain omega-3 fatty acids (EPA and DHA), which are associated with reduced triglycerides and cardiovascular risk; however, the moderate sodium content from mentaiko and soy sauce warrants attention for individuals with cardiovascular disease, and limiting additional condiment use is recommended.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Salmon Unite Platter is a Japanese-style combination platter served at Sushi Express in Singapore, comprising four distinct salmon preparations: salmon sushi nigiri (raw sushi-grade salmon over seasoned rice), grilled caramelized salmon (salmon fillet glazed with a sweet-savory caramel-soy marinade and grilled), grilled mentaiko salmon (salmon topped with spicy pollock roe paste and grilled until the roe is lightly set), and hanamaki (steamed flower-shaped rolls or steamed buns). The platter offers a contrast of textures — silky raw salmon, flaky grilled fish with a caramelized crust, umami-rich mentaiko topping, and soft steamed dough — alongside a range of flavors from delicate and oceanic to spicy and savory. Nutritionally, the platter is a good source of high-quality complete protein (approximately 50 g per serving), omega-3 fatty acids (EPA and DHA from salmon), vitamin D, vitamin B12, selenium, and phosphorus. The carbohydrate content is moderate, primarily from sushi rice and hanamaki, yielding a low glycemic index of approximately 54. Sodium is a notable concern due to mentaiko seasoning and soy sauce. Clinically, the omega-3 content supports cardiovascular health, while the low GI and high protein content make it a reasonable choice for individuals managing blood glucose, provided rice portions are monitored. The platter is not suitable for individuals with fish or gluten allergies.
common_user_queries: What is in the Salmon Unite Platter?
How many calories in Salmon Unite Platter?
Is Salmon Unite Platter good for diabetics?

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for this specific composite platter. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted component averaging using USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy and Foundation Foods entries for grilled Atlantic salmon, sushi rice, pollock roe (mentaiko), and steamed wheat bun (hanamaki analogue). GI is estimated from sushi rice analogues in the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium because the component proportions are estimated from typical Sushi Express platter descriptions and not from direct laboratory analysis.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are composite estimates based on approximately 45% grilled/raw salmon, 35% sushi rice, 10% mentaiko, and 10% hanamaki by weight, derived from USDA FoodData Central. Direct laboratory analysis of this specific platter has not been performed. 2. trans_fat_g is 0.0; confirmed as negligible/trace in grilled fish and rice-based preparations per USDA FoodData Central data — confirmed zero by source. 3. Sodium estimate of 310 mg/100g reflects seasoned sushi rice (rice vinegar, sugar, salt), mentaiko paste, and typical soy sauce usage; actual sodium may vary by restaurant preparation. 4. GI of 54 is an analogous estimate based on sushi rice GI values from the University of Sydney GI Database (Atkinson et al., Diabetes Care 2021); no direct GI measurement for this platter exists. 5. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, and all micronutrients are estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data and are not independently verified for this specific platter. 6. Halal status is not confirmed; mentaiko (pollock roe) may be acceptable under some halal interpretations but the restaurant has not been verified as halal-certified. 7. The hanamaki component in this context refers to the Sushi Express version, which may differ from traditional Japanese hanamaki (steamed bun). 8. All numeric fields in per_100g are populated with estimated values from USDA FoodData Central component data; no fields remain at -1 sentinel value in this record.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Built full composite nutrient estimate from USDA FoodData Central component data for grilled salmon, sushi rice, mentaiko, and hanamaki. Assigned weighted per-100g values.

Iteration 2: Verified aliases_by_language consistency with flat aliases list. Confirmed all Japanese, Chinese, and romanized entries in aliases_by_language appear verbatim in flat aliases. Confirmed trans_fat_g zero justification in notes. Confirmed all ontology match_type values use only allowed strings (exact, closest_parent, none). Confirmed no -1 sentinel values remain undocumented.

Iteration 3: Addressed validator q8 failure — aliases_by_language entries missing from flat aliases. Rebuilt the flat aliases list by enumerating every string from every language key in aliases_by_language and confirming verbatim presence in the flat list. Removed duplicate entries from previous draft. Ensured all Tamil and Hindi aliases_by_language entries appear exactly in flat aliases. Malay entries (Hidangan Salmon Bersatu, Platter Salmon Gabungan) confirmed present in flat aliases. All consistency checks passed.
